title: EFT: Self-Disclosure
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides for therapist self-disclosure in EFT.

sources.label: Watson and Sharbanee, Emotion Focused Therapy: Case Conceptualization and Treatment

slides.title: Marker: your experience may help
slides.body: Use disclosure only when it supports contact, safety, repair, or experiencing.
bullets: A relational moment needs transparency to remain authentic.
The client is guessing your reaction and losing contact.
A brief disclosure could validate reality or repair a miss.
If the disclosure meets your need, do not use it.

slides.title: Make it brief, relevant, and return it to the client
slides.body: In EFT, therapist genuineness supports the client's experiencing; it should not become a second focus of care.
steps: Check your motive: repair, contact, validation, or process guidance.
Disclose the smallest truthful piece needed for the clinical purpose.
Use present-moment language: what I notice in me as I sit with you.
Immediately ask how it lands in the client's body or meaning-making.
Track whether it opens experience, closes experience, or shifts care onto you.
Repair quickly if the client feels burdened, exposed, or managed.

slides.title: Decision filter
rows: It asks the client to manage you.
Reduces shame and returns to experience.
